description: Laws protecting homebuyers and regulating real estate developers and projects in India.

sections.title: Registration of Real Estate Projects
sections.text: No promoter shall advertise, market, book, sell or offer for sale, or invite persons to purchase any plot, apartment or building without registering the real estate project with RERA.
sections.simplified: No builder/developer can sell or advertise a property without first registering the project with RERA. If a builder is selling without RERA registration, it is illegal.
sections.punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years and/or fine up to 10% of project cost

sections.title: Builder's Obligations on Registration
sections.text: The promoter shall disclose: project layout, plan, development works, proposed facilities, proforma allotment letter, agreement for sale, carpet area, number of garages, sanctioned plans, names of contractors and architects, and timeline for completion.
sections.simplified: Once registered, the builder MUST disclose everything: layout, plans, carpet area, contractor details, and most importantly — the project completion timeline. Any false information is punishable.

sections.title: Obligations to Adhere to Sanctioned Plans
sections.text: The promoter shall not make any additions or alterations in the sanctioned plans, layout plans and specifications without the previous written consent of at least two-thirds of the allottees.
sections.simplified: The builder cannot change the approved plans (layout, design) without consent of at least 2/3 of the buyers. If the builder changed plans without your consent, you can complain to RERA.

sections.title: Refund on Builder's Default
sections.text: If the promoter fails to complete or is unable to give possession of the apartment in accordance with the agreement, the allottee shall be entitled to: (a) withdraw from the project with full refund including interest, or (b) continue with interest compensation for delayed delivery.
sections.simplified: If the builder delays possession beyond the promised date, you can either: (1) Get a full refund with interest, or (2) Continue and receive interest compensation for the delay. The interest rate is set by RERA.

sections.title: Return of Amount and Compensation
sections.text: If the allottee is not satisfied with the quality of the apartment or the promoter fails to hand over possession, the promoter shall be liable to compensate the allottee. Structural defects within 5 years must be rectified at the promoter's cost within 30 days.
sections.simplified: If there are structural defects in your flat within 5 years of possession, the builder MUST repair them for free within 30 days. If they don't, you can file a RERA complaint for compensation.

sections.title: Filing Complaint with RERA Authority
sections.text: Any aggrieved person may file a complaint with the RERA Authority for any violation or contravention of the provisions of this Act by the promoter, allottee or real estate agent. The Authority shall endeavour to dispose of the complaint within 60 days.
sections.simplified: You can file a complaint with the RERA Authority against your builder for any violation — delayed possession, plan changes, defects, etc. RERA should resolve it within 60 days. You can also appeal to the Appellate Tribunal.

sections.title: Rights of Allottees (Homebuyers)
sections.text: The allottee shall be entitled to: (i) obtain information relating to sanctioned plans, (ii) know stage-wise completion status, (iii) possession of apartment as per agreement, (iv) claim the possession of apartment on the date specified, (v) refund with interest on failure of the promoter.
sections.simplified: As a homebuyer, you have the right to: see all project plans, know construction progress, get possession on time, demand refund with interest if builder defaults, and get accurate carpet area measurement.
